mechanism_narrative: A downward trajectory in the federal funds rate reduces the cost of capital and increases the present value of future corporate cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman correlation between month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears and downward earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated demand and supply contraction that amplifies downside risk to earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of month-over-month RSAFS and INDPRO declines does not produce a negative Rank IC exceeding 0.04 against 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates above nominal growth expectations, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240 and FEDFUNDS is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating new residential housing construction signals durable consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum fails to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with falling unemployment signals a productive capacity utilization increase that supports margin expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO growth and UNRATE declines does not achieve a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day forward returns over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative payroll growth signals contracting aggregate income and consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not exhibit a positive Rank IC with 60-day forward returns exceeding 0.03 over any 5-year rolling period.
